title: Multiplicative (Luong) Attention
concept: Luong attention calculates similarity using a dot product (or matrix multiply) between query and key vectors, which is faster than additive attention.

exercises.prompt: What is the main goal of an attention mechanism in AI?

options.text: To help the AI focus on relevant parts of the input
To make the model completely ignore past words
To slow down the training process on purpose

exercises.explain: Attention mechanisms allow models to focus on specific, relevant parts of the input sequence when generating output.

cards.title: Need for Speed!
cards.body: We know attention helps AI focus on the right words. But how does it calculate *what* to focus on? Luong Attention uses a clever math shortcut—multiplication—to do this focus calculation at lightning speed!

cards.title: What is Luong Attention?
cards.body: Luong Attention (also called Multiplicative Attention) is a method that calculates how well a Query and Key match by multiplying them together (using a dot product). Because computers are incredibly fast at multiplying matrices, this method is much quicker than adding vectors together.

cards.title: The Dictionary Search
cards.body: Imagine you are looking up a word in a dictionary. In Additive Attention, you compare your word to every entry by carefully reading a detailed description of how they match. In Luong (Multiplicative) Attention, you just instantly snap your fingers and do a quick 'matching score' math trick (like a direct dot product) to see if they line up. It is way faster!

cards.title: Luong Attention in Action
cards.body: When translating a sentence like 'The cat sleeps' into Spanish, the AI creates a query vector for the word it is writing. It multiplies this query vector with the key vectors of all input words. The word 'cat' gets the highest multiplication score, so the AI focuses on it to write 'gato'.

cards.title: Is Faster Worse?
cards.body: Some think that because Luong Attention is simpler and faster, it must be less accurate than Additive Attention. In reality, both perform incredibly well, but Luong's mathematical simplicity makes it the preferred choice when speed is a priority!

cards.title: Comparing Attention Styles
cards.body: Let's compare how Additive and Multiplicative (Luong) attention calculate similarity scores between queries and keys.

left.text: Adds vectors together and runs them through a small neural network. Slower.

right.text: Multiplies vectors directly using a dot product. Much faster.

exercises.prompt: What is another common name for Luong Attention?
exercises.explain: Luong Attention is known as Multiplicative Attention because it uses dot products (a form of multiplication) to compare vectors.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Luong attention is generally faster to compute than additive attention because computers are optimized for matrix multiplication.
exercises.explain: This is true! Luong Attention uses multiplication, which modern hardware (like GPUs) can process extremely quickly.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to complete the description of Luong Attention.
exercises.sentence: Luong attention calculates the similarity between queries and keys using matrix multiplication instead of addition.
exercises.explain: Luong Attention is also called multiplicative attention because it relies on multiplying vectors (like a dot product) rather than adding them.

exercises.prompt: Identify the system that is using Luong (Multiplicative) Attention.

options.text: System A: It calculates similarity by feeding the vectors through an additional neural network layer.
System B: It calculates similarity using a direct dot product (multiplication) between vectors.
System C: It calculates similarity by randomly choosing which words to focus on.

exercises.explain: The second system is using Luong attention because it uses a direct dot product (multiplication) to calculate similarity scores, which is faster.

exercises.prompt: Why is Luong (Multiplicative) Attention generally faster to compute than Additive Attention?

options.text: It uses multiplication (like a dot product), which is much faster to compute.
It uses addition, which requires more complex math formulas.
It completely avoids using keys and queries to save memory.
It processes words one by one in a slow, single-threaded loop.

exercises.explain: Luong attention uses a multiplicative (dot product) method to calculate attention scores. Because this is just a quick matrix multiplication, it runs much faster than Additive (Bahdanau) attention, which requires calculating a slower non-linear function.
